reason: Destination uses path.basename() of files filtered by strict YYYY-MM-DD regex inside a CLI runtime, so no '../' or absolute path can reach path.join.
JSON.parse runs in a local /scripts/ build tool on a baseline file read from disk, not a request body or external stream.
plistPath is derived by resolveLaunchAgentPlistPathForLabel from operator env/label in a local daemon setup writing to ~/Library, not user-controlled web input.
The !== 'none' check compares a categorical tokenSource discriminator string to a literal, not a secret/token value, so no timing leak applies.
URL is a validated camera-node https URL (host-pinned, no auth/token semantics) interpolated into a local CLI download error, not a credentialed API URL.
video.url from external AI provider response is fetched directly without hostname allowlist or validation against the configured provider host.
This is a release verification script under scripts/lib invoked by maintainers with operator-trusted CLI args and known clawhub registry URLs, not a runtime request handler.
session.cwd is explicitly wrapped in sanitizeForPromptLiteral() before being interpolated into the prompt line.

path.resolve operates on tokens parsed from a local node_modules .cmd shim wrapper file, an operator-installed trusted source, and the candidate is gated by isFilePath; no untrusted user input flows here.
new URL is used only to parse a dispatcher-path URL against a localhost base for query extraction; no fetch is made with this URL, so SSRF does not apply.
All header values are passed through the formatForLog() neutralization helper before interpolation into the log string.
fs.realpathSync is used for an approval-boundary script operand, but only isFile()/sha is checked; stat.nlink === 1 is not verified, matching the rule's hardlink-bypass pattern.
The output path is resolved via resolveWritableOutputPathOrRespond(rootDir,...), the project-standard root-scoped writable-path helper, before being passed to traceStopViaPlaywright.
new RegExp() arguments come from marker.pattern.source/flags where marker iterates over the hardcoded INLINE_MARKERS constant defined in the same module, not user input.
This is the exported `testing` (__testing) processMessage wrapper for an operator-authenticated Zalo account context, not an externally exposed webhook handler.
Code aggregates a Record of provider env-var name lists for manifest metadata; it never writes values into process.env, so the dangerous-env-name rule does not apply.
The writeFile truncates an already-existing session file (hadSessionFile guard true) so the prior 0o600 mode is preserved; surrounding writes also pass {mode:0o600}.

applyEventAuthModeToSenderGate intentionally downgrades a failed sender gate to allowed:true with effect:'ignore' only for non-inbound events that are authorized by other gates (command/origin/route), as the comment documents.

The listener.handle just forwards to runDiscordReactionHandler -> handleDiscordReactionEvent, which receives dmPolicy/allowFrom/groupPolicy and calls authorizeDiscordReactionIngress; auth is performed downstream on the same path.

client.sendMessage is the internal Matrix transport layer using a roomId resolved by resolveMatrixRoomId from the caller-supplied target; target authorization is the responsibility of upstream callers, not this low-level send helper.
Function delegates non-wildcard fall-through to resolveAllowlistMatchByCandidates which returns allowed:false on miss; empty list also explicitly returns allowed:false at line 81.
Write occurs in a developer/CI regression script (scripts/check-gateway-watch-regression.mjs) targeting an operator-controlled outputDir, not production runtime.

Boundary check uses startsWith on path.resolve without realpath; though inputs are internal manifest-derived, the rule's literal pattern matches and symlinks under bundledPluginsDir could bypass the check.
--no-sandbox appears in scripts/dev/realtime-talk-live-smoke.ts, a developer smoke-test script, not production runtime.
Title and uri are sanitized via escapeResourceTitle() and escapeInlineControlChars() before interpolation, so attacker-influenced metadata is already escaped.
Function returns a safe restrictive default sandbox mode ('workspace-write') when no allowed mode matches; this is deny-by-default-to-safe-mode, not a missing deny.
safeRemoteHost and safeRemotePath are validated by normalizeScpRemoteHost/normalizeScpRemotePath and rejected if falsy, and the spawn uses '--' separator plus StrictHostKeyChecking=yes.
The path.resolve(root, relative) result is immediately passed to assertSandboxPath which enforces workspace containment before fs.mkdir.
The template literal interpolates only the module-level constant FTS_TABLE into a DROP TABLE statement on a local sqlite handle; no user/untrusted input is in the command.
handleMessage is a private client-side WebSocket message dispatcher in browser UI processing replies from an already-authenticated Google Live session, not an unauthenticated webhook endpoint.
targetGlobalRoot is derived from package-manager probe output (npm/pnpm/bun global root) joined with a constant PRIMARY_PACKAGE_NAME — not user-controlled input requiring containment.
tokenSummaryPath is a QA token-efficiency metrics report (JSON of throughput stats), not a credential/secret file — rule misfired on the substring 'token'.
The RegExp source is built from a hardcoded const tuple of literal tag names ['system-reminder','previous_response'] joined with '|' — no user input flows into the pattern.
realpath is used inside resolvePathForContainment specifically to canonicalize before containment comparison — that is the symlink-defeating mechanism, lstat is not the right guard here.
'token' here is a CLI argv token from readTrimmedArgToken being compared to literal strings ('exec','dlx','node','--'), not a secret/auth token — no timing attack surface.
Build/doc-generation script (.mjs in scripts/) writing to repo-controlled paths under ROOT/REFERENCE_DIR, not production runtime nor user-controlled paths.
URL resolves to Anthropic's API endpoint from operator-configured baseUrl (default trusted), used for outbound provider API call with API key auth, not user-controllable SSRF input.
The new URL(params.url) is immediately used to derive allowedHostnames passed to fetchWithSsrFGuard, which is the SSRF guard itself - this is the helper enforcing the allowlist.
File is monitor-handler.test-helpers.ts importing vitest and creating mock activity handlers for tests; not a production reaction handler path.
res.end(error.message) writes upstream connection error message directly into the proxy response body, leaking internal error details to the client.
Passes 'isolated: false' literal to ensureCodexPluginActivation request, disabling sandbox isolation for migration apply.
Loop assigns providerId/keys from plugin manifest entries into a normalized envVars map for provider auth lookup; no shell/process env mutation occurs.
attachment.label is rendered into an attribute via lit-html template for UI display, not interpolated into an LLM prompt.
URL is a public npm/CDN release verification URL in a build/release script, not user-credentialed; release-beta-verifier is operator tooling under scripts/.
isAllowedInteractionSource returns true unconditionally when allowedSourceIps is empty/undefined, defaulting to allow when the allowlist is unconfigured.
jid is an internal parameter to an outbound send helper called from application dispatch flow, not externally-controlled input requiring per-call validation.
JSON.parse runs on Twilio REST error response text (trusted upstream API), wrapped in try/catch with shape validation; no untrusted DoS vector.
targetPath is built internally via buildClobberedTargetPath from app config path; writeFile uses exclusive 'wx' flag and 0o600 mode, no user-controlled traversal.
'host' here is account.config.webhookHost (operator-controlled config), not an HTTP request header; rule misidentified the source.
new URL() inside stripUrlCredentials is pure parsing to strip user:pass from a URL string; no fetch/SSRF surface where an allowlist would apply.
